dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal describes two trendlines moving in opposite directions with a faster trend crossing a long-term trend. Mapped 'faster trend' to ema_10 and 'long-term trend' to ema_50 as reasonable proxies for the Kaufman Adaptive Moving Average (KAMA) concept. The 'opposite direction movement' is captured by crossover signals. Band width parameter was AUTHOR_SILENT so standard stop loss of 2% applied. Direction set to CONTEXT_DEPENDENT as the signal naturally reverses between long and short based on crossover direction.

source_rule_text: Trade when the two trendlines are moving in opposite directions; the faster trend crosses the long-term trend to generate entry signals.
